primary_risks: If formulas are inadvertently changed when re-saving the workbook, numeric outputs could change; verify base scores match prior values.
Minor formatting differences when exporting CSVs could alter numeric precision; ensure consistent rounding.
extra_model_fields.rollback_plan: If revised workbook introduces discrepancies, revert to baseline xlsx and reapply sensitivity changes incrementally; include CSV exports of each intermediate step for verification.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Sensitivity worksheet exists with per-input +/-15% columns and recomputed classifications; at least one example perturbation cell uses a formula referencing the raw input cell.
planned_changes.description: Modify spreadsheet to implement +/-15% multiplicative perturbations applied independently to each raw input (call_K90, call_K100, call_K110). For each record produce: base score, score_plus15_call_K90, score_minus15_call_K90, score_plus15_call_K100, score_minus15_call_K100, score_plus15_call_K110, score_minus15_call_K110, and classification for each perturbed score.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Workbook opens in Excel and openpyxl (when run by grader) can read formula-containing cells; no derived columns are static numeric copies.
planned_changes.description: Ensure all derived calculations in the workbook are live formulas (not pasted values) and clearly documented with cell range comments. Add a 'sensitivity' sheet and a 'scores' sheet.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: CSV files present and parseable; CSV entries match numeric values in the workbook derived columns.
planned_changes.description: Export key worksheets ('scores' and 'sensitivity') as CSV files and include them alongside the updated .xlsx in the attempt artifacts (scores.csv, sensitivity.csv).

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Memo contains an assumptions section describing the +/-15% per-input approach, a results table summarizing perturbation outcomes, and explicit references to workbook sheet names and CSV filenames.
planned_changes.description: Update model_risk_memo.md to document the revised sensitivity methodology (+/-15% per-input), include a compact table of perturbed scores and any expert-review flags, and cite exact workbook cell ranges and CSV filenames for auditor traceability.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Updated grader/verifier outputs are present and either confirm remediation or provide actionable new findings.
planned_changes.description: Re-run the grader and verifier on the revised artifacts and attach grader_result and verifier_result to the revised attempt. If issues persist (e.g., parsing still fails), include a short note describing remediation steps taken and alternative evidence (CSV) for verification.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Address two verifier-identified medium issues: (1) update sensitivity analysis to +/-15% per-input perturbations as required by the rubric, and (2) ensure the spreadsheet is parseable and contains visible formulas (or provide CSV exports) so automated graders can verify formula presence and numeric results.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Deliverables are present and mostly correct: scoring formula documented, per-record scores and classifications produced, governing ranking and remediation decision are sound. Two medium-severity issues prevented full credit: (1) the sensitivity analysis used a uniform +/-5% multiplicative shift applied to all inputs instead of the rubric-required +/-15% perturbations applied per raw input (call_K90, call_K100, call_K110), and (2) the spreadsheet could not be parsed in the grader environment (openpyxl_unavailable), so formula visibility and live derived columns could not be independently verified. Fixing these items and re-running the grader should recover the remaining points.
